title: Support class:: constant function references in elaboration
body:
In the following code:

```SystemVerilog
module test;
    virtual class C #(parameter a = 0);
        static function zero ();
            return 0;
        endfunction
    endclass

    localparam b = C#(0)::zero();
endmodule
```

`verilator --lint-only test.sv`

```
%Error-UNSUPPORTED: test.sv:9:27: dotted expressions in parameters
                                : ... note: In instance 'test'
                                : ... Suggest use a typedef
    9 |     localparam b = C#(0)::zero();
      |                           ^~~~
```
I see the use of this message in [t_class_param_nested_bad.out](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/3752102879c4545a372c2a21142385df9965fef2/test_regress/t/t_class_param_nested_bad.out#L4) only in context of dotted reference in a class parameter list, so maybe a false positive? `Wrap2 #(Wrap#(19)::PBASE * 2) w38;`

If `b` is not a parameter (e. g. `logic`): `logic b = C#(0)::zero();`, the error goes away. If class `C` is not parameterized, there's no error either.

Version:
`Verilator 5.045 devel rev v5.044-154-gbbb231dfe (mod)`

OS:
Arch Linux

I'm planning on learning C++, but it would probably take months before I'll be able to understand what's going on and try to fix it.
